Looking at the history:
- Step 61: Clicked (12,37) - gray diamond became white, orange at (14,29)
- Step 62: Clicked (10,44) - gray diamond became white, orange still at (14,29)
- Step 63: Clicked (14,29) - white segment became gray, orange moved to (15,24)
- Step 64: Clicked (21,8) - gray diamond became white, orange still at (15,24)
- Step 65: Clicked (15,24) - white segment became gray, orange moved to (13,30)
- Step 66: Clicked (13,30) - orange stayed, segment at (15,24) became gray
- Step 67: Clicked (12,37) - gray diamond became white, orange stayed at (13,30)
- Step 68: Clicked (46,30) - orange diamond disappeared
- Step 69: Clicked (46,52) - gray segment at (43,50) became white
- Step 70: Clicked (46,52) - no change
Key observations:
1. Clicking the orange square makes it move along the path (step 63: 14,29 -> 15,24; step 65: 15,24 -> 13,30)
2. Clicking gray diamonds makes them white
3. The orange square moves when you click it, but only if there's a path segment to move to
The orange square is at (13,30). Looking at the path, the next segment might be at (12,37) which is white. But clicking the orange square at (13,30) didn't move it (step 66).
Wait, let me reconsider. The path segments are:
- (21,8) - white
- (15,24) - gray (was white, became gray when orange moved from it)
- (13,30) - orange square is here
- (12,37) - white (was gray, became white when clicked)
- (10,44) - white (was gray, became white when clicked)
The orange square moved from (14,29) to (15,24) when clicked at (14,29), and from (15,24) to (13,30) when clicked at (15,24). But it didn't move when clicked at (13,30).
Maybe the orange square can only move to adjacent path segments that are gray? At (13,30), the adjacent segments are (12,37) which is white and (15,24) which is gray. But the orange square didn't move to (15,24).
Actually, looking more carefully, the orange square moved from (14,29) to (15,24) which is down-right, and from (15,24) to (13,30) which is up-right. So it's moving along the path.
The path seems to go: (21,8) -> (15,24) -> (13,30) -> (12,37) -> (10,44)
Or maybe: (21,8) -> ... -> (15,24) -> (13,30) -> (12,37) -> (10,44)
The orange square is at (13,30) and needs to reach (10,44). The next segment is (12,37) which is white. Maybe I need to click the white segment at (12,37) to make it gray, then click the orange square to move it there?
Actually, looking at step 67, clicking (12,37) made it white (it was gray before). So clicking a gray segment makes it white, and clicking a white segment might make it gray?
Let me try clicking (12,37) to toggle it, then click the orange square to see if it moves.
Wait, but I need to be more careful. Let me look at the pattern:
- Step 61: Clicked (12,37) which was gray -> became white
- Step 62: Clicked (10,44) which was gray -> became white  
- Step 63: Clicked (14,29) which was white -> became gray, orange moved to (15,24)
- Step 64: Clicked (21,8) which was gray -> became white
- Step 65: Clicked (15,24) which was white -> became gray, orange moved to (13,30)
- Step 66: Clicked (13,30) which is the orange square -> no move, (15,24) became gray
- Step 67: Clicked (12,37) which was gray -> became white
So the pattern is:
- Clicking a gray segment makes it white
- Clicking a white segment makes it gray AND moves the orange square to that position
- Clicking the orange square itself doesn't move it
So to move the orange square from (13,30) to (12,37), I need to:
1. Make (12,37) white (it already is)
2. Click (12,37) to make it gray and move the orange square there
Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me re-read:
- Step 63: Clicked (14,29) which was white -> became gray, orange moved to (15,24)
  - So clicking a white segment makes it gray and moves the orange square to that segment's position? No, the orange moved to (15,24), not (14,29).
Hmm, let me re-examine. At step 62, the orange was at (14,29). At step 63, I clicked (14,29) and the orange moved to (15,24). So clicking the orange square's position makes it move to the next segment on the path.
At step 64, the orange was at (15,24). At step 65, I clicked (15,24) and the orange moved to (13,30). So clicking the orange square's position makes it move to the next segment.
At step 65, the orange was at (13,30). At step 66, I clicked (13,30) and the orange stayed at (13,30). So why didn't it move?
Maybe the orange square can only move if the next segment on the path is white? Let me check:
- At step 63, the orange moved from (14,29) to (15,24). Was (15,24) white before step 63? Looking at step 62, (15,24) is not listed, so it might have been black or part of the background.
Actually, I think I'm overcomplicating this. Let me just try clicking the orange square at (13,30) again and see what happens.
Wait, I already did that at step 66 and it didn't move. Let me think about what's different.
Looking at the path segments:
- (21,8) - white
- (15,24) - gray
- (13,30) - orange square
- (12,37) - white
- (10,44) - white
The orange square is at (13,30). The next segment on the path toward the end might be (12,37) which is white. But clicking (13,30) didn't move the orange square.
Maybe I need to click the target segment (12,37) to make the orange square move there? Let me try clicking (12,37).
